summary: Iteration 17 — Verified the two bug fixes (TEST OTP email verification + Google graceful failure) against the SHARED PRODUCTION Firebase project 'trademate-new' + Atlas DB 'leadnation'. Backend: 3/3 pytest cases PASS (~9s) in /app/backend/tests/test_otp_verify.py — created throwaway uid via Firebase REST accounts:signUp (otpqa_<ts>@leadnation.test) → allocated customer_id 00010 via /api/onboarding/register; POST /api/auth/request-otp returned {ok:true,test_mode:true,message:'...123456...'}; POST /api/auth/verify-otp {otp:'000000'} → 400 'Invalid verification code'; POST /api/auth/verify-otp {otp:'123456'} → {ok:true,verified:true}; GET /api/auth/me showed user.is_email_verified=true AND a fresh Firebase accounts:lookup showed emailVerified=true (both sides flipped). Frontend (Playwright): /signup created otpui_1782768808@leadnation.test → /account allocated customer_id 00011, status 'Not verified', account-verify-card visible. Wrong code '999999' → account-verify-msg shows 'Invalid verification code', status stays 'Not verified'. Correct code '123456' → status flips to 'Verified', account-verify-msg shows 'Email verified ✓' (token refresh + reload worked). /login with krivajexim@gmail.com / Krivaj@12345 → /account shows customer_id 00009 and status 'Verified'; logout works. /login → click data-testid='login-google' → a Google popup opens (no crash, page remains fully functional — login-form still mounted and input editable after click); the googleErr() helper code path was not triggered in this headless run because Chromium allowed the popup. Admin regression: /admin-login with 00001 / Shiv@12345 → /admin-cms loaded (Dashboard/Content/Leads/Service Requests/Events/Control Center/Brain tabs visible). Both throwaway accounts (00010 + 00011) were HARD-DELETED (Firebase + Mongo) before finishing — counters advanced to seq 11.
critical_code_review_comments: accounts.verify_otp uses a hardcoded TEST_OTP via env var — fine for the test phase, but the response 'message' in /auth/request-otp leaks the literal code '123456' to ANY authenticated client. Acceptable while in test_mode, but ensure this branch is REMOVED before a live OTP provider goes live (or gated behind a TEST_OTP_ENABLED flag) so production never advertises the bypass code.

accounts.verify_otp accepts ANY length string in OtpBody.otp — consider Pydantic constr(min_length=4, max_length=8, strip_whitespace=True) and a numeric-only check; today '000000\n' would already be normalised by .strip() but '12 34 56' or non-digits would silently 400 with the same message.

Auth.jsx googleErr() returns generic 'Google sign-in failed' as the fallback for any error code other than the three known ones; on unauthorized-domain in some Firebase SDK versions the code may surface as 'auth/operation-not-allowed' or 'auth/internal-error' — consider broadening the substring match (e.g. include 'operation-not-allowed' and 'internal-error') to keep the user-facing copy actionable.

Auth.jsx /account verify card always renders 'Send verification code' as a TEXT link (data-testid='account-send-otp') — works, but lacks a loading/disabled state during the request; double-click could trigger two /auth/request-otp calls.

AuthContext.verifyOtp performs auth.currentUser.reload() + force-refresh of the ID token — good; consider also re-fetching /auth/me afterwards so the local 'account' state is fully reconciled (today the verified flag flips via fbUser.emailVerified, but server-side is_email_verified updates would not show until next page load if something else later toggled it).
test_otp_verify.py fixture leaks the throwaway customer_id to stdout on teardown — intentional for cleanup visibility; if this file gets pushed to CI, redact the email/uid from logs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 flows: signup+OTP wrong+OTP right, 00009 login, Google graceful, admin regression)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@leadnation.app / Shiv@12345 (00001). Verified user: krivajexim@gmail.com / Krivaj@12345 (00009). Firebase Web API key from /app/frontend/.env. TEST_OTP=123456 from /app/backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Two throwaway users created and BOTH HARD-DELETED in the same run: (1) backend pytest → customer_id 00010 / otpqa_1782768743@leadnation.test (deleted by fixture teardown); (2) frontend /signup → customer_id 00011 / otpui_1782768808@leadnation.test (deleted via DELETE /api/admin_v2/users/00011/hard-delete with admin token). _counters seq advanced to 11 — IDs are never reused, expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both bug fixes are working end-to-end against shared production Firebase + Atlas. To re-verify backend quickly: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_otp_verify.py -v -s` — self-creates a throwaway, exercises request-otp / verify-otp wrong / verify-otp right / /auth/me / Firebase lookup, then HARD-deletes via admin. The verifyOtp client flow in AuthContext.jsx does auth.currentUser.reload() + getIdToken(true) — give the UI ~5s after clicking account-verify-otp before reading account-verified-status because the Firebase reload + token refresh + React state propagation is async. Google popup CANNOT be automated headlessly — only verify (a) clicking login-google does not crash the page, (b) login-form is still mounted afterwards, (c) IF the popup is blocked the login-error testid shows googleErr() copy. Do NOT delete 00001 (protected, 403 by design) or 00009 (real customer). When testing /signup or onboarding, ALWAYS HARD-DELETE the throwaway customer_id at the end so DB doesn't bloat (counter increment is permanent, that's expected).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Both fixes work: (1) /api/auth/verify-otp uses TEST_OTP=123456 from env; on match it calls firebase_auth.set_email_verified(uid) AND updates Mongo users.is_email_verified — both sides flip atomically; the client-side AuthContext.verifyOtp() then does auth.currentUser.reload() + getIdToken(true) so the local fbUser.emailVerified is fresh, and the Account page reads `fbUser.emailVerified || account.user.is_email_verified` so the status flips immediately without a page reload. (2) Auth.jsx googleErr() helper maps Firebase error codes ('auth/unauthorized-domain', 'auth/popup-blocked', 'auth/popup-closed', 'auth/cancelled-popup-request') to friendly messages routed into data-testid='login-error', and any unknown code falls back to a generic 'try email/password' message — the page no longer crashes, the form remains usable. In the headless Chromium run, the popup actually opened (Playwright allows popups by default) so the unauthorized-domain branch wasn't triggered — but the critical assertion (page remains functional after Google click, form still editable) passed. The two minor flakes from iteration 16 (post-logout race, signup-role select) are not present in this run because the script waits 3s after logout and uses select_option for the role dropdown.
